title: API Resources, Object Identity, Schemas, Desired State, and Status · Reconciliation Brief: K8s Spec Status Generation
summary: A shared cluster rejects and defaults manifests differently across supported API versions while stale status leads a release team toward the wrong repair. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s spec status generation increment in versioned API object contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate desired spec, controller-owned status, metadata generation, observedGeneration, and status conditions before judging convergence.
Resolve API group, version, kind, plural resource, scope, endpoint, and served or stored version for a concrete object.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example manifest is valid YAML but uses a removed API, contains an unknown field, and reports observedGeneration 2 behind generation 3. Establish discovery, schema, identity, spec, status, conditions, and compatibility evidence. Competency probe: Separate desired spec, controller-owned status, metadata generation, observedGeneration, and status conditions before judging convergence. A defensible solution applies K8s Spec Status Generation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to versioned API object contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-spec-status-generation — Separate desired spec, controller-owned status, metadata generation, observedGeneration, and status conditions before judging convergence.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-api-objects-schema-status-4
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-api-objects-schema-status-4
    artifact: api-object.yaml
    decision: worked k8s api objects schema status 4
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      replicas: 3
      status-evidence: observedGeneration-and-conditions
    verify: kubectl explain deployment.spec --api-version=apps/v1 && kubectl get deployment status-api -o json
    failure: Reject valid-YAML-only evidence, stale status, unknown fields, or an API selected without feature-state and deprecation review.
    repair: Use live discovery and schema, separate spec from status, compare generation with observedGeneration, and preserve object UID and resourceVersion.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-spec-status-generation-1pw3nb
  - competency: k8s-spec-status-generation
    artifact: api-object.yaml
    decision: spec status generation
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: withdraw the selected image digest after one node pulls it
    retained-proof: bound claim snapshot checksum and application consistency query
    case-key: 1pw3nb
    change: |
      apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      replicas: 3
      status-evidence: observedGeneration-and-conditions
    verify: kubectl explain deployment.spec --api-version=apps/v1 && kubectl get deployment status-api -o json
    failure: Reject valid-YAML-only evidence, stale status, unknown fields, or an API selected without feature-state and deprecation review.
    repair: Use live discovery and schema, separate spec from status, compare generation with observedGeneration, and preserve object UID and resourceVersion.
